rationale.D1: Confirmed full IO layer across all game subsystems (IntakeIO, HopperIO, ShooterIO, SwerveDrivetrainIO, VisionIO) with hardware impls (IOTalonFX) and null-object fallbacks, selected in RobotContainer's switch; no generic servo base or replay IO variants to justify 4.
rationale.D2: ShooterModes acts as a mode-state coordinator and CrossSubsystemsCommandsFactory fans shooter/hopper/intake interactions via mode-driven triggers and command groups, but there is no named Superstructure with a goal-request API and guarded transitions — closer to explicit per-subsystem state with cross-subsystem orchestration than a true level-3 coordinator.
rationale.D3: Lib contains ArmSystemSim, ElevatorSystemSim, FlywheelSystemSim, and VisionIOSim (used for sim robot config), and a SIM run mode exists; however the competition game mechanisms (Shooter, Intake, Hopper) have no physics-backed IO implementations — sim-mode uses the anonymous null-object IO, so mechanism physics sim is not confirmed in use.
rationale.D4: No src/test directory exists; no test files or assertion calls found anywhere in the repo.
rationale.D5: AdvantageKit is fully wired: @AutoLog inputs structs on all subsystems, Logger.processInputs called in every subsystem periodic, LoggedTunableNumber/LoggedDashboardChooser throughout, replayWatch Gradle task present, REPLAY mode wired with null-object IOs in RobotContainer, and FaultReporter system-check commands provide operational diagnostics — confirmed D5=4.
rationale.D6: 63 PathPlanner path files with AutoBuilder/PathPlannerAuto/PathPlannerPath confirmed in AutonomousCommandsFactory, plus LocalADStarAK for AK-integrated pathfinding and a DriveToPose on-the-fly drive-to-pose command; no Choreo trajectories but on-the-fly pose driving confirms level 3.
rationale.D7: VisionIO interface with VisionIOPhotonVision, VisionIONorthstar, and VisionIOSim implementations; addVisionMeasurement wired through RobotOdometry singleton with std-dev tuning and multi-camera fusion confirmed in Vision.java; strong level 3, one step short of a full world-model with TimeInterpolatableBuffer.
rationale.D8: Comprehensive README with build/deploy instructions, links to tuning/configuration docs, and reference videos; Spotless google-java-format enforced in build.gradle; AdvantageScope.json and AdvantageScopeTuning.json layouts committed; multi-robot config system; lib package carried across seasons; no .github/workflows directory found in this repo clone, so CI cannot be confirmed.
